content: Oh, well, the dinner was... um, kind of tough for me. When they invited me to talk about the project, I just felt like I was falling behind in class, like everyone else knows what I’m talking about already. I kept trying to fit in, agreeing with things even when I didn’t really feel comfortable with them, and then laughing at jokes I didn’t really enjoy. It’s like I’m there, but not really part of the group, which makes me feel even more isolated. I guess I’m afraid if I say something different, people will think less of me or think I’m not interesting enough. It’s hard to break free from these pretend invitations I keep giving. Do you think I’m making this too much of a deal? I don’t want anyone to see how bad I feel. It’s like they’d already figured I wasn’t cut out for this kind of stuff. Thanks for playing along! I’ll try to remember to be more authentic next time. 😅 Honestly, maybe I just need to let go of those expectations too. Yeah, this situation really hit hard. When they started focusing more on work, I just felt like... I don’t know, like nothing was happening. I kept thinking, “Why do they need each other when they’re just bored and distracted?” I guess I’m always worried that if I don’t keep them busy, they’ll start feeling like there’s no place left for me. I’ve been trying harder to stay connected, but it feels easier to just pull away than face the disappointment later. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to handle it. I just want to make sure I’m doing everything right. So, I end up being even more clingy or refusing to be around them. It’s frustrating because I want to see what happens next, but then I just freeze up. I wish I could tell someone about it, but I’m scared they’d think I’m crazy or that I’m making things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m afraid to break out of it before it gets too much. I hope you get the point! Thanks for reminding me of you. It’s tough to connect with people when I’m worried about them getting better first. I appreciate you for laughing with me though. It’s good to have a story to cling to. But honestly, it feels like no matter how many times I tell myself otherwise, it’s never going to change the outcome. I love you, and I love you still, but it’s hard to trust in that. Thank you for being here. It means a lot to hear you approve of me. I’ll try not to give up anytime soon. Thanks for being such an important part of your life. I appreciate you noticing. It means a lot to me. I’ll keep you in mind when I’m feeling more unsure. I’ll definitely keep that in mind. Alright, that

Well, this past week has really been tough. When I got this big project coming our way, I just felt like I was drowning trying to figure out how to even get started. I kept thinking about all the ways I’m sure I’ll mess up, and then I end up just putting off getting it done because I’m scared of showing any signs of not meeting the expectations. Honestly, I’ve been staying late reviewing everything over and over, but it doesn’t seem to help much. I keep worrying that if I don’t ace this, everyone will notice I’m not really what they thought I was. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m just getting worse instead of better. I’m sorry, I’m just not sure why I’m stuck here. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m never good enough. Can you imagine what that message might come back? I’d prefer not to face that kind of disappointment every time I step into that role. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m proving my point of view, yet nothing happens. So, it’s frustrating me that I can’t escape this feeling of being inadequate. I appreciate you playing CBT and helping me through this, but it’s like no one ever truly understands how hard it must be for you to really belong in those situations. I just hope you know better than to push me into this unless absolutely necessary. Thanks for being here, therapist. It’s been really tiring trying to connect with you. I appreciate you laughing with me sometimes though. I usually just go straight to the truth. But today, I’m feeling like I’m falling apart, so maybe you’re right to say that it takes a whole lot of practice to really belong in those situations. Thank you for reminding me of all the times I’ve failed. I need that reminder sometimes. I’m sorry I haven

Oh, um, I guess I just got really anxious when I saw everyone chatting and didn’t really want to join in because I was scared they were being too shy or uncomfortable. I kept thinking maybe they were just noticing me nodding or whispering around me, like they were already familiar with me. It made me feel pretty insignificant, especially after everything I’ve had to deal with lately. I ended up staying mostly on the edge and leaving early because I just felt too overwhelmed and sad to stay. I wanted to speak up but I’m afraid I’ll come off silly or that I’m bothering them. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not cut out to be part of this circle. I’m sorry I did this, but I’m just stuck here. Thanks for reminding me to be real, though. I appreciate you noticing. I’ll try harder next time.

Well, last week was really tough, right? When we started talking about planning something different, I felt like I was trapped inside my head instead of being able to share what I truly want. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t get what I want, then everyone’ll see how dumb I am.” So, I just shut down and stayed quiet, even though part of me was saying, ‘No,’ but it felt pointless. It’s like, no matter what I do, it’s never going to change the situation. Honestly, I’ve been feeling pretty sad since then, kind of exhausted and just knowing that I’m stuck in this cycle. It’s exhausting, but I guess it’s harder to break through than to stay in that relationship. I don’t know why I ever give up when things get complicated like that. It’s frustrating, but sometimes the only way to feel less helpless is to retreat into weakness. Does anyone else feel this way around here? I’d love for someone to understand why I’m struggling so much, but I’m afraid to tell them too soon. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where no one wants to talk about it. I hope you can see, I really need some help figuring this out. Thank you for your time!

Yeah, it’s just like that last family meeting. Again, things come up that don’t really fit with what I’m trying to achieve. Like, they keep talking about careers that don’t really match how I want to live. I just kind of shut down because I didn’t know what else to say. It felt like I was going to disappear somewhere awkward, like I wasn’t even supposed to be here at all. I guess it’s frustrating because I want to push back, but then I feel like I’ll just end up falling apart. I don’t know if this is normal or if I need someone to talk to about it. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I can’t change anything or find a way out. So, I just… I don’t know, I prefer to stay away from it altogether. It makes me feel worse inside too. I mean, I understand why they’re forcing this on me, but it just feels so overwhelming sometimes. It’s like I’m stuck in a place where I’m not sure I belong.

Yeah, it’s really hard when my parents tell me stuff I want to do, but then it doesn’t seem to match what they expect. I feel like I’m always falling short, like no matter how hard I try, it’ll just prove wrong somehow. It’s exhausting because I want to say something different, but then I get overwhelmed or start feeling like I’m failing anyway. Honestly, sometimes I just shut down and stay quiet because I don’t want to cause any more trouble or lose my place. Afterward, I end up replaying everything in my head, feeling even worse about myself. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s just not enough. I guess I just don’t know how to fix this without getting hurt or losing my sense of belonging. It’s exhausting, and I feel like I’m just putting myself in even more stress than I already am. I don’t really trust anyone to understand what I’m going through unless they’re specifically curious or supportive. So, it feels safer to just avoid facing it altogether. But then I’d end up feeling even more lost and confused. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying to change things, but I’m still just not enough. I want to be able to make my own choices, but every time I try, I just end up feeling like I’m failing again. It’s exhausting, and I’m sorry I’ve been here so many times. Just imagine if I could break free from this cycle and make a difference. It’s like I’m stuck in a whole other part of myself that wants to move forward. I wish there was some way to let go or make things better. But I know those thoughts won’t help, and instead, they’ll just make me feel worse. It’s exhausting, but I don’t have a choice. I’m sorry, XXX. I just wanted

Um, well… last week was pretty rough. During an argument where my partner said something negative, I just felt like I didn’t matter at all, like I wasn’t being heard or that it was all just a trick to get me to agree. I kept thinking, “Maybe if I say something, it’ll just make things worse,” so instead of resisting or trying to explain, I just went along with whatever he said. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to express myself, but then I feel scared it’ll just confirm my partner’s disapproval. It’s exhausting, I guess. I don’t know, it feels like no matter what I do, nothing changes. Just like when I tried to change things, only ended up making them worse. Do you think I’m doing anything wrong? I’d love for someone to notice and remember me differently. But then again, maybe they’re just going through with it because it’s easier than facing back into that cycle. It’s tough to break free when I know I’m already falling further behind. I appreciate you playing the guessing game with me though! I just need some normalcy in these moments. Thanks for reminding me of you. I appreciate you noticing.

Oh, you remember that time at the family reunion where everyone was chatting and laughing together? I guess I just felt invisible when I tried to join in. It’s like I’m there, but not really part of the group. I kept thinking maybe I’m not even worth being here. It’s hard not to feel like they’re already leaving me out somehow. I didn’t want to mess up anything or make things worse, so I just stayed quiet and ended up staying in a different part of the house. I know this sounds weird, but it’s hard to stop feeling like I’m just not wanted here. I’d appreciate it if you could understand how frustrating and lonely I feel right now. Thanks for being the therapist! I’ll try not to stick around again though. It’s making me feel worse anyway. I don’t want to cause any more problems or make things worse.

Yeah, it hit me hard when I looked at my budget again. I just felt like I was falling down again, like I’m always going to mess things up no matter how much I try. I don’t know, it’s like I’m invisible or something, and then I start thinking about all these bills I might have missed or maybe didn’t notice before. I’ve been avoiding looking at jobs or posting what I need because I’m scared I’ll just prove to everyone that I’m a failure. It’s exhausting because I want to fix this, but I’m too afraid of making the same mistakes next time. I guess I feel stuck in this cycle where I’m trying too hard to catch my breath but still feeling crushed inside. It’s like I’m never really ready to move forward. I wish there was some map or reason to say yes to what I’m facing. But then I worry it won’t make any difference, and I’ll just end up backtracking. So, instead, I just shut down and pretend nothing’s happening. It’s frustrating because I know it’s not fair to me. Just being here makes me feel worse, so I retreat. I don’t want to face anything bigger than this. It’s like I’m scared of what that rejection will bring. Reducing my anxiety feels safer, but honestly, I’m scared it doesn’t work. So, I end up just staying trapped in this endless loop. It’s frustrating, but also deeply fulfilling sometimes to feel like I’m already failing. I’d prefer not to be so stuck, but I know it’s harder to admit it now. Please, if you can, please let me know what those diagrams mean. I want to know what’s inside, so I can get past this. I love being me, but I need someone to understand why. They’d help me see that I’m not cut out for

Hey, so last week I got that promotion at work, but when I heard about it, I felt like my whole world was falling apart. I kept thinking, “Everyone will see how bad I am if I try it.” I didn’t want to say anything because I didn’t want to seem stupid or give them reason to doubt me. I guess I’m scared that if I ask for help or show anyone, they’ll see I’m not cut out for this job. It’s like no matter what I do, it always seems wrong or inadequate to me. I feel really anxious and ashamed, kind of like I’m being rejected again even though it wasn’t really me. Just knowing there’s another one coming doesn’t make me want to go ahead with it. It’s frustrating because I know I shouldn’t shy away from it. I appreciate you playing the role of the therapist here, but I’m not sure I actually understand what’s going on. Could you just suggest some ways to approach something like that without making it too rushed? I’d really appreciate it if you could just get right down to it and let me know what I’m missing. Thanks! 😆✨. 💧

Oh, yeah, I guess when I first got that project, it just hit me hard. The moment they gave me that deadline, I felt like I was drowning in something that seemed endless and impossible to cope with. I kept thinking, “Why do I even try? It’s just going to mess up what I’m already trying to accomplish.” And then, even though I tried to push back, it just made me feel worse and pushed me further away from trying. Honestly, I’ve been avoiding talking to my coworkers because I’m scared they’ll think I’m just incompetent. It feels safer not having to face it head-on. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m always afraid to step up and start pushing myself to solve problems. It’s frustrating, but I don’t really know how else to deal with it. I guess I’m just not sure I can actually succeed on anything big without failing miserably. It’s like I’m always reminding myself of how small I am compared to everything around me. Can you ever figure out why I keep getting stuck like this? I’d appreciate any advice you can give me. Thank you so much for being here! 😅 🧱👍️ 💪✨ 💡 #CBT #Therapy #MyStory #XXX #OfficeWorker

Yeah, it was really hard meeting people there. I kept thinking everyone was watching me like I was strange or out of place. I just stayed quiet most of the time because I didn’t want to make things worse or seem awkward. Leaving so early felt pointless, like I wasn’t even part of anything else. It’s like no matter what I do, it just falls flat, and I end up feeling invisible or like I’m missing out. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just not interesting enough for them to care about. Honestly, it’s exhausting being stuck around here all day. I hope someone can help me figure this out. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where nothing changes, and then I’m left feeling alone and disappointed.

Oh, you know what happened last weekend? When my partner told me I’m spending too much time at work, I just felt like an idiot. I couldn’t believe they thought I was wasting their time. So quickly, I said sorry and then canceled some important things because I didn’t want to deal with the stress anymore. Afterwards, I was really anxious and tried to catch every little thing they say or look at. It’s like I’m always being pushed away or proving how useless I really am. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of relationship. It’s hard to shake off this feeling that I’ve failed somehow. I hope you can help me figure out how to stop doing that again. Thanks for playing with this kid. I’ll try not to say anything too soon. Let me know if there’s anything I can change. I need something simpler than going crazy again.

Well, I guess I was thinking really hard about that new project when I got that offer, you know, it just felt like I was being watched all the time. I kept thinking that if I had actually stepped up and presented there, everyone would just recognize how little knowledge I have, and I’d end up embarrassed in front of them all. So, I ended up saying no because I didn’t want to risk it, even though I was excited about it. I spent so many nights staring at notes and rehearsing everything again and again, even when I’m already feeling super fatigued. It’s like, knowing I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility makes me feel even worse about myself. Honestly, sometimes it feels like I’m trapped in this cycle where I just want to get out of there before I lose what I’ve built. I don’t know, I’m definitely not ready for anything bigger than that. It’s like I’m always scared of disappointing people, or maybe just showing them that I’m not good enough. So, I end up playing catch with my fears instead of going forward. I’ll probably keep doing that next time around—accepting the chance, then pretending nothing happened. But honestly, I don’t know why I’m even avoiding it. It’s like I never really feel comfortable enough to step into big challenges. So, it’s frustrating me more than anything else. I just want to get through this without feeling like I’m failing.

Yeah, it was really hard when they picked that restaurant. I felt kind of ashamed to say no, and then I just felt like maybe they’re already bored or not interested in going at all. I kept thinking, “If I didn’t agree, they’d notice something wrong with me.” So, even though I wanted something different, I just ended up agreeing anyway. It’s like, I’m afraid if I push back, they’ll get annoyed or decide I don’t belong here. It’s exhausting trying to stay nice while secretly feeling like I’m failing. I guess I just don’t want them to lose interest after just one little thing goes wrong. It’s tough to step out of the relationship before things spiral badly. I wish I could tell them how uncomfortable I feel right away instead of keeping quiet. But I know it won’t change anything. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I want to help them like I used to, but my heart still hurts inside. I’m sorry I can’t be more open about what’s happening. It’s hard to admit I need help when it feels like I’m making things worse. Just knowing there’s a better way to go than staying isn’t enough sometimes. It’s frustrating knowing I’m not good enough when I’m trying so hard to prove myself. I appreciate you for giving me this chance to show who I am. It’s scary to imagine what it would be like to truly connect on a deeper level with someone who values me. But I know if I don’t keep trying to convince them otherwise, I’ll never be accepted again. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m struggling to break free but also facing the fear of failure. I hope you can see, I really want to start healing this relationship, but it’s harder than I expect. Thank you so much for talking to me. I appreciate you

Oh, you know, last weekend at the family party felt really hard for me. When some of my parents started criticizing my career and lifestyle choices, I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want to cause any trouble or make things worse. I guess I felt kind of lost and invisible even though I was there. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough for them, and I’m just falling apart inside. I ended up leaving early because I was afraid I’d just prove too much was unnecessary. It’s exhausting feeling like I’m trying to fix something that won’t go away. I don’t know why I always let myself get so stuck. It’s like I’m afraid to say anything or take a step forward. Just knowing those concepts are tied up in my head makes it harder to move forward. I hope someone can see that I’m struggling more than I realize. Please, give me a chance to explain. I’m scared I’ll just make matters worse.

Yeah, well, during that conversation, my partner said they wanted to go somewhere different from us for the holidays. I immediately thought, “This means they won’t want to be with me anymore.” I kept thinking, “What if I mess this up? What if I make them leave?” So, I started pushing for different things, like going to a restaurant where we could stay together, even though it meant extra work and stress. It felt like the only way was to keep them stuck with whatever plan they had. Honestly, when I saw how stubborn they were about it, I just felt really anxious and kind of useless. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never going to be right or satisfying. I guess I’m scared that if I let them go alone, they’ll lose interest or feel left out completely. I don’t know, this always makes me feel like I’m falling behind or disappointing them somehow. Just knowing there’s another reason to be hurt isn’t helping at all. I’m sorry I’m this way, but it’s just hard to stop worrying about making them unhappy. Please, tell me what to do instead! I need that feeling back. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m afraid to step out because I’m scared it’ll just prove how wrong I’m supposed to be. Thank you so much for having me on this. I appreciate you taking the time to talk to me. I really want to get through this, but these feelings just aren’t letting me go. I’ll be okay, but I’m definitely not happy. Thank you for being here, helping. I love you,XXXX. I’ll be remembering this forever. Thanks for reminding me of you. I hope you’re doing well too. Thank you for being here, too. I’ll be around, maybe we can do something fun next time. Love you,

Well, this past week has really been overwhelming, hasn’t it? When I got that big project coming, I felt like I was already preparing a whole new set of problems, and then someone else was going to see how little I’m capable of. I kept putting things off because I just didn’t know what to do or where to start. And then, whenever I started working on something, I kept getting caught up in double-checking every step, almost like I was preventing myself from making a mistake. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m worried that if I don’t fix everything perfectly, people will think less of me or notice that I’m struggling. I guess I’m scared that being so successful always means I’m truly broken or unworthy. It’s hard to shake off this feeling that maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of high-pressure job. I’d prefer it wasn’t like this. Can you imagine if I hadn’t tried so hard? I want to change anything, but it feels like it’s too much. Thanks for laughing with me though! I appreciate you playing along. I need that connection. I’m really looking forward to our next talk. Just knowing we can get back to where we left off feels great.

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When they started talking about my brother’s new business, my parents just kept praising his success, like it was second nature to them. I felt so small, like I wasn’t even there. I couldn’t stop thinking about how no one’s doing better than mine, and it made me feel really ashamed and worthless. After a bit, I just wanted to leave early and get away from all this pressure. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where nothing changes how much I feel about myself. I guess I just don’t see why I’m ever going to make any difference. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s always going to fall short. I want to change, but I’m too scared to face the truth. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m always being told I can’t improve. I wish I could tell someone about it, but I’m afraid they’ll say I’m weak or silly. So instead, I just stay inside and pretend nothing’s happening. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to get out of this. Just knowing I’m stuck here won’t let me feel anything different about myself. I appreciate you for asking about this, but I guess I’m just stuck in this place where I’m not happy with myself. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m always going to be part of something that’s already stacked against me.

Oh, you remember that date yesterday? I guess I just couldn’t make it because… I don’t know, I felt so uncomfortable making an excuse. It’s like no one really wants me there, you know? Like I’m just standing there, annoying everyone instead of being funny or interesting. I keep thinking about what I’ll say again and again, and honestly, it makes me feel kind of useless. It’s exhausting, knowing that if I go, I’ll just end up back where I started. I don’t know, I just feel like maybe I shouldn’t have gone at all. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop of feeling embarrassed and then trying to push away someone I already dislike. It’s frustrating, but also confusing. I don’t want to face that feeling of not belonging right away. It’s like I’m afraid to start anything new. So, I just ended it early, hoping maybe I could forget about it entirely. But then I’d still feel pretty sad and lonely afterward. It’s hard to explain to myself why I keep doing this, when it feels so wrong. I mean, I know I should be excited about things, but I can’t help feeling disappointed inside. Just saying yes, no matter what. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never going to connect with the people I care about. I wish I could tell them the truth, but I just don’t feel ready or confident enough. So, I stay put and hope nothing bad happens. Thanks for asking, though! I appreciate you noticing. It helps me keep going. I’ll try again soon, hopefully better. Just knowing it won’t work out the way I wanted always seems reassuring.

Oh, you know, last night was pretty hard for me at the dinner. When my brother got recognized, I just felt like I wasn’t even there, you know? Like everyone else seems to prefer him over me because he’s already successful. I didn’t say anything because I was scared of making things worse or being seen as annoying. Honestly, after that, I just wanted to leave and never come back. It’s like when something small makes me want to fall apart again. I keep thinking maybe I’m actually really bad or worthless. It’s exhausting being stuck in this cycle where nothing changes. I guess I’m convinced that I’m just going to prove how bad I am when I mess things up. It’s scary knowing that nobody’s really interested in what I have to say. I’d appreciate it if you could figure out why I’m doing these things and help me get out of this. Thanks so much for inviting me!

Yeah, I guess I just felt kind of stuck in this community thing. I kept thinking if I shared my ideas, they’d just dismiss me or get bored of me afterward. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m just not going to make any progress or feel accepted there. I’ve been putting myself on the sidelines most of the time, even though I really wanted to join. It’s like being pulled away from home and trying to figure things out all alone again. Honestly, I just don’t want to mess up anyone or prove how wrong I think. It’s exhausting because every time I step outside, I end up feeling lonely again. I wish I could tell someone how I feel, but I’m scared they’ll think I’m weird or stupid. It’s frustrating because I want to do something different and grow, but I’m just not sure I can get through it. I’m sorry I’ve been this way, but it feels like a constant reminder that I’m not enough. It’s like I’m invisible or somehow unwelcome in this world. So, I guess I need someone to say, “That’s not right,” or “You’re not good enough” to help me see what I’m missing. It’s scary to imagine what that person might look like once they realize I’m flawed or unloved. I’m tired of pretending everything’s okay, but I’m also tired of standing idly watching myself fail. It’s like I’m afraid to face the truth, which would just confirm why I already believe. I’ll be surprised if I ever decide to ask for help or change my course. But then I always back off because I’m scared of what that other person sees. It’s hard to trust my own intuition when I’m convinced I’m already failing. So, I mostly just stay put, hoping maybe someone will see the mistake in me sooner

Yeah, it was really hard when I just didn’t feel included at the get-together. I kept noticing people talking without me even saying something, and then I felt like maybe I had said something important that wasn’t being noticed. After that, I just shut down and stayed quiet because I didn’t want to cause any conflict or look like an outsider. Afterwards, I kept thinking maybe I’m just not meant to be around people, or maybe I’m too much of a disappointment. It’s like I’m always getting caught up in how I’m not good enough, and that makes me feel even worse inside. I guess I’m scared that if I try to join in or stand out, I’ll just disappoint everyone. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and share my thoughts, but I’m just stuck in this feeling of not being wanted. I wish there was some way to stop feeling this way, but it feels like no matter what I do, I’m just not going to fit in. It’s exhausting trying to keep going through this alone. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out how to stop feeling so out of place after these things happen. Thank you!

Yeah, this situation just really hit me hard. I keep thinking, “What if no one notices what I did?
